channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: DOGE officials home in on Medicare, Medicaid offices
item.description: It was unclear which systems DOGE had access to and whether any sensitive medical information was part of the efforts.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A short honeymoon for Catholics in D.C. as Vice President Vance clashes with bishops on migration
item.description: The sense of Catholic unity didn’t last long. Less than a week after Vice President JD Vance shared the inauguration stage with a senior Catholic cardinal, Vance picked a fight with the top American leaders of his church. The new vice president, a Catholic convert, accused the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: WATCH: Trump attends bipartisan National Prayer Breakfast, a decadeslong Washington tradition
item.description: President Donald Trump participated in the National Prayer Breakfast at the Capitol on Thursday, joining a more than 70-year-old Washington tradition that brings together a bipartisan group of lawmakers for fellowship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Freezing hikers stuck in waist-deep snow rescued during Mount Washington whiteout
item.description: The two friends had hiked New Hampshire's challenging Mount Washington trail a month ago. They returned and did it again Sunday after trekking through neighboring peaks in the White Mountains.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israel begins preparations for Gaza exodus as Egypt lobbies against Trump plan
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel said on Thursday it has begun preparations for the departure of large numbers of Palestinians from the Gaza Strip in line with President Donald Trump's plan for the territory.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: From fighting disease to protecting the Amazon rainforest, USAID has big impact across the globe
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to close the U.S. Agency for International Development has drawn widespread criticism from congressional Democrats and raised questions and concern about the influence billionaire ally Elon Musk wields over the federal government.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ukraine says its long-range drones hit a Russian airfield as France delivers Mirage fighter jets
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian forces struck an airfield in southern Russia that was being used to launch Iran-designed Shahed drones, Ukraine’s General Staff claimed Thursday, as France said it had delivered a first batch of Mirage fighter jets to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 5 things to watch as the federal buyout deadline approaches
item.description: As the deadline approaches, agencies have amped up the pressure on employees, sending frequent “reminders” and a copy of the contract they would have to sign to accept the deferred resignation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boy Scouts see a small membership uptick after rebrand to Scouting America
item.description: IRVING, Texas (AP) — A historic rebrand of the Boy Scouts of America has been followed by a small uptick in young people joining what will now be called Scouting America, a welcoming sign as the organization tries to move past scandal and bankruptcy.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mexican troops begin arriving at US-Mexico border following deal made to pause Trump-approved tariffs
item.description: Mexico has started deploying soldiers to its northern border in accordance with the 10,000 troops it promised to send to the border under a deal made with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas school district warns parents Border Patrol may question or deport students
item.description: Parents across Texas are being warned that Border Patrol agents may be boarding school buses and their children may be detained or deported if they can not prove citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about what's tying up a $4B settlement for Hawaii wildfire victims
item.description: The Hawaii Supreme Court is scheduled to hear about insurance issues tying up a potential $4 billion Maui wild fire settlement.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Sinco Kelleher, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US to seize Venezuelan president's plane held in Dominican Republic during Rubio visit
item.description: The Trump administration plans to seize the second of two planes belonging to Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro's government that is currently in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: CIA shares new employee names through unclassified email
item.description: The CIA used unclassified email to share a list of employees hired within the last two years to the Office of Personnel Management (OPM).
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Texas to execute man who killed pastor in church days after being released from anger management program
item.description: Texas will put to death on Wednesday a man convicted of the brutal 2011 killing of a Dallas-area pastor in his own church during a robbery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big Game Bound: Pros detail offensive matchup battle in Super Bowl LIX
item.description: When it comes to offense, both the Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles are firing on all cylinders heading into Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: Honolulu records 6th death from massive explosion of illegal fireworks over New Year's
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A sixth person died Wednesday from injuries sustained when crates of illegal fireworks ignited during a New Year’s Eve party in a Honolulu neighborhood, setting off a chain of explosions that left more than a dozen people with severe burns.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New NASA imagery shows a large asteroid could impact Earth in 2032
item.description: A recently released animation shows a new look at a large asteroid that has a small, but significant, chance of impacting planet Earth in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KTLA 5

item.title: Democrats accuse DOGE of going after NOAA
item.description: Democratic lawmakers on Wednesday accused Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) of improperly inserting itself into the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). In a joint statement, Reps. Zoe Lofgren (D-Calif.) and Jared Huffman (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chinese migrant on paddleboard with suitcase in tow apprehended near Bahamas days after separate Florida bust
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard found and apprehended a Chinese migrant floating on a pair of paddleboards off the Bahamas, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan Airlines plane clips Delta flight at Sea-Tac airport
item.description: A plane at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport hit another aircraft that was taxiing on the runway Wednesday morning, the Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Storm threatens significant ice accumulations in several mid-Atlantic states, forecasters say
item.description: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — A winter storm approaching several mid-Atlantic states is threatening significant ice accumulations that could cause widespread power outages and disrupt travel, forecasters warned Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s birthright citizenship order is put on hold by a second federal judge
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday ordered a second nationwide pause on President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for anyone born in the U.S. to someone in the country illegally, calling citizenship a “most precious right.” U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Delphi murders: State's filing includes photos of Libby German's iPhone 6s
item.description: In November, a jury convicted Allen of murder in the February 2017 deaths of Abby Williams and Libby German near the Monon High Bridge.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Jeremiah Trotter Jr. continues legacy of Eagles Super Bowl linebackers
item.description: His dad, known as the "Ax Man" for his signature sack celebration, played eight seasons in Midnight Green and is a member of the team's Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma board denies clemency for man on death row, clears path for March execution
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — An Oklahoma board rejected clemency on Wednesday for a man sentenced to die for fatally shooting a woman during a 2005 home-invasion robbery, clearing the way for him to become the first person executed in the state this year.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Authorities nab White House fence climber just two weeks into Trump's new term
item.description: U.S. Secret Service took someone into custody after the individual sought to scale the White House fence on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bondi sworn in as attorney general in Oval Office
item.description: Pam Bondi was sworn in as attorney general on Wednesday in the Oval Office with President Trump looking on and telling reporters she would be “as impartial as a person can be." “I know I'm supposed to say, ‘She's going to be totally impartial with respect to Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A timeline of last week's air disaster in Washington, the deadliest in the US since 2001
item.description: It has been nearly a week since a commercial jetliner and an Army helicopter collided in midair near Washington’s Ronald Reagan National Airport, killing 67 people in the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr. pass committee hurdles in win for Trump
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Tuesday cleared crucial hurdles toward winning Senate confirmation, handing President Trump a key win as he looks for an early display of loyalty from GOP lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims in deadly DC plane and chopper collision now recovered
item.description: The chief medical examiner is still trying to positively identify one set of remains, officials said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Oregon Zoo welcomes a new baby elephant
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — The Oregon Zoo in Portland has welcomed its newest addition, a baby elephant. Thirty-year-old Asian elephant Rose-Tu gave birth on Saturday after 20 months of pregnancy, the zoo said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump suggests displaced Palestinians in Gaza be 'permanently' resettled outside war-torn territory
item.description: The renewed call by the Trump administration on Arab nations to relocate displaced Palestinians comes as Trump is set to meet with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the White House later Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Church vandalized by Proud Boys now controls group's trademark, judge rules
item.description: The Proud Boys is barred from selling merchandise using its name or symbols without permission from a Black church once vandalized by the right-wing extremist group's members, after a Washington, D.C., judge on Monday revoked its control over the trademark.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Passengers, crew killed in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia identified
item.description: Authorities said a seventh person, who had been in a car at the time of the crash, has died. Their identity has not yet been released.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9
